lesson_objective: A variable's type is locked in the moment you declare it, and Java enforces that before your code ever runs.
lesson_summary: This lesson shows that declaring a variable in Java means committing to a type up front, using a labeled storage bin analogy, and demonstrates the two ways that type gets enforced: a compile-time mismatch and a silent overflow.
key_points: A variable is a named, typed storage location, not just a label.
Java checks the type at compile time, not runtime — a mismatch won't even build.
Even a 'valid' type can misbehave at its edges, like int silently overflowing.
step_by_step_explanation: Open on the labeled storage bin analogy: a bin marked 'books' won't accept a couch.
Show declaring an int, a double, and a boolean, each holding a value of its declared type.
Attempt to assign a String literal to the int variable — show the compiler rejecting it before the program ever runs.
Contrast: show int overflow (Integer.MAX_VALUE + 1) compiling and running fine, but producing a silently wrong number — a mismatch of expectation, not of type.
Land on the takeaway: type safety catches one class of bug at compile time, but not every bug — you still have to know your numbers' limits.

core_example.description: Three variable declarations (int, double, boolean) followed by one line that fails to compile from a type mismatch.
core_example.focus_note: Highlight the final line and the resulting compiler error message — that's the moment the lesson's point lands.

analogy: A variable is like a labeled storage bin at a warehouse: the label says the bin can only hold books, and the warehouse simply won't let you shelve a couch there.
common_mistakes: Assuming Java infers a variable's type from its value instead of requiring an explicit declared type.
Expecting a type mismatch to be a runtime surprise rather than a compile-time error.
what_to_avoid_saying: Do not say Java 'infers' the type of a normal variable declaration — that's only true for var, a separate later concept.
Do not say int overflow throws an exception — it silently wraps around.
beginner_takeaway: If it compiles, the type is already right — but that doesn't mean the value inside it is safe from every kind of mistake.
retention_hook: Java won't let you put a couch in a bin marked 'books' — and it checks before you even try to lift it.
visual_notes: Highlight the compiler error line in red the instant the mismatched assignment appears on screen.
Show the storage bin visual behind the three variable declarations as they're introduced.
